Plumber in Orland Park, IL

Licensed, Bonded & Insured • Population 58,590 • Cook County • 24/7 Service

Call 833-758-6911
Home › Service Area › Cook County › Orland Park, IL
Licensed & Insured 24/7 Emergency Service Serving Orland Park, Cook County

Orland Park is one of the largest and most commercially active villages in Chicago's southwest suburbs. Our licensed plumbers serve both residential neighborhoods and the extensive commercial corridor with comprehensive plumbing services.

Orland Park's development spans several decades, creating distinct plumbing profiles across the village. Established neighborhoods from the 1970s and 1980s — particularly near the Orland Square area and along 151st Street — feature homes with aging copper supply lines, cast iron drain systems, and clay sewer laterals that are increasingly due for inspection and replacement. Meanwhile, newer subdivisions in the western portion of the village have more modern PVC and PEX systems but may face settling-related issues as the formerly agricultural land continues to compact.

The village's major commercial corridors along LaGrange Road and 159th Street include extensive retail, restaurant, and office properties that require specialized commercial plumbing services including grease trap installation and maintenance, backflow preventer testing, and high-volume drain systems. Our commercial plumbing team handles these specialized requirements for Orland Park businesses.

Orland Park receives Lake Michigan water through a regional system, providing generally reliable service and quality. The village requires plumbing permits for most work, and our licensed plumbers manage the permit and inspection process to ensure code compliance.

Local Plumbing Information for Orland Park

Water Utility
Village of Orland Park — Water Division (Lake Michigan water via Oak Lawn regional system)
Permits
Orland Park requires plumbing permits through the Development Services Department. Inspections are required for all permitted work.
Neighborhoods Served
Orland Square area, Homer Township, Crystal Tree, Centennial Park, Old Orland Historic District

Common Plumbing Issues in Orland Park

Frequently Asked Questions — Orland Park Plumbing

What plumbing services do Orland Park homeowners need most?

Orland Park has a large stock of homes built in the 1970s–1990s. The most common service calls are water heater replacements (units reaching end of 10–15 year life), sewer line cleaning and camera inspection, sump pump repairs, and fixture upgrades during kitchen and bathroom remodels.

Does Orland Park have hard water?

Orland Park receives Lake Michigan water, which is moderately hard. While not as hard as well water communities, mineral buildup still affects water heaters and fixtures over time. Some homeowners opt for water softeners or conditioning systems.

How much does a sewer line replacement cost in Orland Park?

Sewer line replacement in Orland Park typically runs $5,000–$15,000 for traditional excavation or $6,000–$18,000 for trenchless methods. Costs depend on pipe length, depth, landscaping impact, and whether the line runs under a driveway or patio.

Do you handle plumbing for Orland Park businesses?

Yes. Orland Park has a thriving commercial district around Orland Square Mall, LaGrange Road, and 159th Street. We provide commercial plumbing services including restaurant drain maintenance, backflow testing, and emergency response for retail and office spaces.

Can you remodel a bathroom in an Orland Park home?

Yes. We handle all plumbing aspects of bathroom remodels — fixture installation, shower valve replacement, drain and supply line relocations, and tub-to-shower conversions. We coordinate with your general contractor or work directly with you on plumbing-only projects.
